original: "Frozen", the highest-grossing animated film of all time, starringIdina MenzelandKristen Bell, is released
Berners Street hoax: Theodore Hook bets he can make any address the most talked-about in London, proceeds to win by bringing London to a standstill
Mauna Loa, the world's largest active volcano, erupts for he first time in nearly 40 years on Hawaii island
In New York City, the first Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade is held
"Holy grail" of shipwrecks the San Jose, sunk 1708, is confirmed found by an international team off the coast of Colombia
North Korean ghost ship washes up Akita prefecture beach, Japan with 8 skeletons on board, 4th boat in a month
8 Donkeys freed from jail after 4 days in Orai, Uttar Pradesh, India for eating plants
Bangkok Fire and Rescue Department confirm it has had 31,801 call outs to deal with snakes this year
Swedish chemistAlfred Nobel's will establishes the Nobel Prize
Hydrogen atmosphere is discovered on the extrasolar planet Osiris by the Hubble Space Telescope, the first atmosphere detected on an extrasolar planet
1st photograph of a meteor taken by Austro-Hungarian photographer Ladislaus Weinek in Prague, Austrian-Hungarian Empire
John Walkerinvents friction matches in England
Soviet Mars 2 becomes 1st spacecraft to crash land on Mars
1st rocket to intercept an airplane at White Sands, New Mexico
Norway opens the world's longest road tunnel - the Laerdal tunnel linking Oslo and Bergen at 15.3-miles (24.6-kilometers)
In highest-scoring NFL game, Washington Redskins defeat NY Giants 72-41
Audience throws vegetables at actors for 1st recorded time in US
Portuguese Royal Family and its court of nearly 15,000 people leave Lisbon for their colony of Brazil to escape invading Napoleonic troops

Christopher Columbusreturns to La Navidad colony, finding it destroyed by the first Native American uprising against Spanish rule. Taíno Cacique Caonabo led his people to attack the settlement after the brutal treatment they received from the garrison who disobeyed Columbus's orders.
Frances Goodrich and Albert Hackett's "The Diary of Anne Frank" premieres in Netherlands
First American superhero film, "The Mark of Zorro," directed by Fred Niblo and starringDouglas Fairbanks, is released in New York

Filming starts for "Star Trek" pilot "The Cage"; cast includes Jeffrey Hunter, Susan Oliver,Leonard Nimoy, and Majel Barret; although it never airs, some footage re-used in "Menagerie" episode of the series
NY's Penn Station opens as world's largest railway terminal
The Beatles release their album "Magical Mystery Tour" in the US; issued as a double EP in the UK in December
Gordie Howebecomes 1st NHLer to score 1,000 points
Trailing 38-7 late in 3rd quarter, Buffalo Bills tie Broncos at 38-38
Freight trains kills more than 100 reindeer over three days in Norway
Ghana celebrates the "year of return" marking 300 years since 1st African slave sold in America, by granting 125 people citizenship in special ceremony
US Army withdraws from Little Rock, Arkansas after Central High School integration
US 63rd manned space mission STS 33 (Discovery 9) returns from space
Jimi Hendrixheadlines bill, supporting acts include The Move andPink Floyd, for 2 shows at Whitla Hall, Queens College, in Belfast - his only concerts in Ireland
Buffalo Bob Smith [Robert Emil Schmidt], American TV host (Howdy Doody), born in Buffalo, New York (d. 1998)
Kevin Henkes, American children's book writer/illustrator (Caldecott Medal), born in Racine, Wisconsin
James Avery, American actor (Fresh Prince of Bell Air), born in Pughsville, Virginia (d. 2013)
Stephanie Gilmore wins her 6th women's world surfing title
Bobby Morrowtakes out the Melbourne Olympics sprint double when he wins the 200m gold medal in equal world record 20.75s; beats American teammates Andy Stanfield and Thane Baker
New Zealand's All Blacks thump Six Nations Rugby Union Champions France 45-6 in Paris
India defeats West Indies in Bengal Jubilee Cricket Final, Kumble 6-12
1st day-night one-day cricket international, Australia v WI at SCG
Kepler Wessels scores century in 1st Test Cricket (162 v England)

Marco Antonio Barrera beats Erik Morales by majority decision to win WBC super featherweight boxing title at the MGM Grand Garden Arena, Las Vegas; final fight of legendary trilogy; The Ring's 'Fight of the Year'
Lions' Barry Sanders becomes NFL's second all-time rusher

German Red Bull driverSebastian Vettelfinishes 2nd in season-ending Brazilian Grand Prix at Autódromo José Carlos Pace to retain his F1 World Drivers Championship; wins by 122 points from Jenson Button
Roger Federerclaims a record 6th ATP World Tour Finals tennis title with a 6–3, 6–7, 6–3 win over Frenchman Jo-Wilfried Tsonga in the final in London, England
Gordie Howebecomes the first to play in 1,000 NHL games
MLB St. Louis Cardinals outfielder Bake McBride wins NL Rookie of Year
Only win ever for NFL's Dallas Texans (1-11), beating the Chicago Bears, 27-23 at the Rubber Bowl in Akron, Ohio
English soccer team beats Netherlands, 8-2
Soyuz T-3 carries 3 cosmonauts to Salyut 6 space station, launched
French navy at Toulon scuttles ships & subs so Nazis cannot seize them
Dutch resistance fighter Hannie Schaft re-buried in presence ofQueen Wilhelmina
Apple Records releasesGeorge Harrison's triple album set "All Things Must Pass" - it becomes the best selling solo release of any of the Beatles, going 6X Platinum
German Mercedes driver Nico Rosberg clinches his only F1 World Driver's Championship with victory in season ending Abu Dhabi Grand Prix; wins title by 5 points from teammateLewis Hamilton
Albanian President Bajram Begaj grants citizenship to pop starDua Lipafor promoting the country through her international fame and her musical talents
The New York Times dubs baseball "The National Game"
Honda 1st opens in America
Restoration of Williamsburg, Virginia, begins
Albanian National Flag adopted
English KingEdward Icalls what later becomes known as "The Model Parliament," extending the authority of its representatives
Uruguay adopts constitution
